conjecture_title: Open question on counter-examples to ℓ(G)+br(G)≥|G|
paper_title: A new class of graphs that satisfies the Chen-Chvátal Conjecture
summary: The structural question of whether all counter-examples to $\ell(G)+\mathrm{br}(G)\geq|G|$ arise, up to bridge-to-path replacement, from a finite base set of graphs remains unresolved in the published literature found. Subsequent work has characterised counter-examples in restricted graph classes (bipartite graphs by Matamala and Zamora 2020, locally connected and diameter-2 graphs circa 2025) and proved the Chen-Chvátal conjecture for graphs of diameter three (arXiv:2512.12047), but none of these results directly address the bridge-elongation characterisation question posed in the source paper.

since_posted.title: Chen-Chvátal Conjecture for Graphs of Diameter 3
since_posted.claim: Proves that all graphs of diameter three satisfy the Chen-Chvátal conjecture by classifying all such graphs having fewer lines than vertices; does not directly address the bridge path elongation characterisation of counter-examples.

notes: The 2020 paper 'Lines in bipartite graphs and in 2-metric spaces' by Matamala and Zamora (J. Graph Theory 94(5):565–585) characterises all bipartite counter-examples to the inequality and is likely the closest related work, but its URL was not WebFetched and it is not included in since_posted. Similarly, 2025 preprints characterising counter-examples for locally connected and diameter-2 graphs were identified in search summaries but not individually verified. No paper that directly resolves the bridge path elongation question was found.
